name: grep
description: Search file contents using ripgrep. Returns matching lines with optional context around each match. Use this when looking for function definitions, variable usages, imports, error messages, or any text pattern inside files. Do not use this for finding files by name (use glob instead) or exploring directory structure (use tree instead). Results are sorted by modification time (newest first) and truncated at the max limit.

pattern.description: Search pattern. Interpreted as regex by default. Use literal=true for exact text matching without regex interpretation.

path.description: Directory to search in. Must be an existing directory path. Defaults to current working directory if not specified.

type.description: File extension filter without the dot (e.g., 'go', 'py', 'ts', 'js'). Repeatable: specify multiple times to include multiple file types.

literal.description: If true, treat pattern as literal text without regex interpretation. Use when searching for exact strings like function calls or error messages.

max.description: Maximum number of matching lines to return. Results are truncated if exceeded. Default: 100.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match. Default: 0 (no context).

name: glob
description: Search for files by name pattern using fd. Returns a list of matching file or directory paths. Use this when you need to find files by extension, name, or pattern (e.g., '*.go', '*_test.go', 'config*.yaml'). Do not use this for searching inside file contents (use grep instead). Results are sorted by modification time (newest first).

path.description: Directory to search in. Must be an existing directory path. Defaults to current working directory if not specified.

type.description: Filter by type: 'f' for files only, 'd' for directories only. If not specified, returns both files and directories.

max.description: Maximum number of results to return. Results are truncated if exceeded. Default: 200.

name: tree
description: Display directory structure as a tree view. Shows folders and files in a hierarchical format with optional depth control and file sizes. Use this to understand project layout, find configuration files, or explore unfamiliar codebases. Do not use this for searching file contents (use grep) or finding files by name (use glob).

path.description: Root directory to display. Must be an existing directory path. Defaults to current working directory if not specified.

depth.description: Maximum depth of directories to display. 1 shows only the root level, 2 shows one level of subdirectories, etc. Default: 3.

size.description: If true, show file sizes in human-readable format (KB, MB) next to each file. Default: false.

name: check
description: Verify that required dependencies (ripgrep, fd, tree) are installed and accessible in the system. Use this before running search commands to ensure all tools are available. Returns success status or lists missing tools.
